original_text: 2011 Ethiopia account ownership was 14% according to Global Findex.
2014 Ethiopia account ownership was 22% according to Global Findex.
2017 Ethiopia account ownership was 35% according to Global Findex.
2021 Ethiopia account ownership was 46% according to Global Findex.
2024 Ethiopia account ownership was 49% according to Global Findex.
notes: Latest Findex Access point showing slowdown.

original_text: Mobile money account ownership was 4.7% in 2021.
Mobile money account ownership was 9.45% in 2024.
notes: Latest mobile money account penetration rate.

original_text: 35% of Ethiopian adults made or received a digital payment in the past 12 months, according to Global Findex 2024.
notes: Core Usage indicator from Global Findex 2024 for forecasting.

original_text: 4G population coverage was 37.5% in mid-2023.
4G coverage is projected to reach 70.8% in mid-2025.
Mobile subscription penetration reaches 61.4% by end-2025.
Fayda digital ID enrollment reached 8 million in August 2024.
Fayda digital ID enrollment reached 12 million in February 2025.
Fayda digital ID enrollment reached 15 million in May 2025.
P2P transaction count was 49.7 million in July 2024.
P2P transaction count reached 128.3 million in July 2025.
notes: Rapid P2P growth after market expansion.

original_text: P2P transaction value was 350 billion ETB in July 2024.
P2P transaction value was 577.7 billion ETB in July 2025.
ATM transaction count was 78 million in July 2024.
notes: Cash channel volume for crossover analysis.

original_text: ATM transaction count was 119.3 million in July 2025.
notes: Cash channel update for crossover analysis.

original_text: ATM transaction value was 95 billion ETB in July 2024.
ATM transaction value was 156.1 billion ETB in July 2025.
P2P/ATM crossover ratio exceeded 1.0 in July 2025.
notes: Key usage milestone showing digital payment dominance.

original_text: Telebirr had 54 million registered users by end-2024.
Telebirr transaction value was 210 billion ETB in 2024.
notes: Transaction value to support usage analysis.

original_text: M-Pesa had 10 million registered users by end-2025.
M-Pesa had 7 million active users by end-2025.
Mobile money activity rate was 66% in 2024.
Data affordability was 26% of income in 2024.
Gender gap in account ownership was 20 percentage points in 2021.
Female share of mobile money accounts was 42% in 2024.
Mobile phone gender gap was 5 percentage points in 2024.
NFIS-II set a 70% account ownership target for 2025.
NFIS-II target for Fayda enrollment is 90 million by 2028.

indicator: Female Mobile Money Account Share Target
original_text: NFIS-II female mobile money account share target of 50% by 2030.
Telebirr launched its mobile money service in May 2021.
notes: Key product launch for digital financial transformation.

original_text: Safaricom entered Ethiopia commercially in August 2022.
M-Pesa launched in Ethiopia in August 2023.
Fayda digital ID rollout began in early 2024.
Foreign exchange liberalization policy was introduced in July 2024.
notes: Macro policy event affecting cost and access.

original_text: P2P volume surpassed ATM volume in October 2024.
notes: Usage milestone reflecting digital payment adoption.

original_text: M-Pesa joined the EthSwitch clearing network in October 2025.
EthioPay instant payment system launched in December 2025.
The NFIS-II strategy was launched in September 2021.
Safaricom raised service prices in December 2025.
Ethiopia digital payment use was very low in early Findex waves; ~2% used digital payments around 2014 (compiled from Findex country tables / secondary reporting).
notes: Historical Usage anchor for sparse-series forecasting.

original_text: Digital payment adoption in Ethiopia remained single-digit in 2017 Findex reporting (~8% made/received digital payments, secondary compilation).
Around 2021, digital payment use among Ethiopian adults was still limited (~18% made or received a digital payment; compiled from Findex 2021 country materials).
notes: Usage baseline near Telebirr launch window.

original_text: Male account ownership in Ethiopia was about 55% in 2021 (Findex gender disaggregation; consistent with ~20pp gender gap vs female).
notes: Gender disaggregation for Access gap analysis.

original_text: Female account ownership in Ethiopia was about 35% in 2021 (Findex gender disaggregation).
notes: Pairs with male ownership to quantify 2021 gender gap.

original_text: Male account ownership remained near mid-50s in 2024 while overall ownership reached 49%.
notes: 2024 male Access disaggregation for gap trend.

original_text: Female account ownership rose toward the low-40s by 2024 as overall ownership reached 49%.
notes: Shows partial closing of gender gap vs 2021.

original_text: Urban adults in Ethiopia have substantially higher account ownership than rural adults (urban ~70%+ in recent Findex waves).
notes: Urban/rural split for Access inequality analysis.

original_text: Rural account ownership remains well below urban rates (~40% vs ~70%+).
notes: Highlights geographic inclusion gap limiting national Access growth.

indicator: Registered vs Survey Mobile Money Gap
source_name: Derived from operator reports and Global Findex 2024
original_text: Telebirr alone reports 54M registered users while survey MM account ownership is only 9.45% of adults — large registered-vs-active/survey gap.
notes: Quantifies dormant/duplicate registration vs demand-side Findex measures.

original_text: Regulatory facilitation of agent networks and simplified onboarding supported mobile money expansion after 2021.
notes: Policy/regulatory enabler for Access and Usage; pillar left empty by design.

original_text: Merchant QR and acceptance expansion accelerated P2P-for-commerce use cases in 2024-2025.
notes: Infrastructure event supporting Usage intensity, not only account opening.
